---
description: "Use when creating social media posts for LinkedIn, X/Twitter, Reddit, Medium, or Substack. Defines platform-specific formatting rules, Unicode character usage, and the visual-first policy for X/Twitter, Reddit, Medium, and Substack."
---

# Social Media Formatting Rules

## LinkedIn (post)

- Text-led, long-form. Use Unicode Mathematical Bold Sans-Serif for emphasis: 𝗕𝗼𝗹𝗱
- Use Unicode Mathematical Italic Sans-Serif for contrast: 𝘐𝘵𝘢𝘭𝘪𝘤
- Separators: ━━━ (full-width horizontal bar)
- Sub-bullets: ▸ (right-pointing small triangle)
- Emoji anchors sparingly: ⚠️📊🎯
- Wrap copy-paste content between `── START COPY ──` and `── END COPY ──` markers

## LinkedIn Article (Step 12)

- Standard Markdown, text-only, **unique angle** — never a republish of the blog
- No image markdown (Google indexes the page; thin visual-only teaser would harm SEO)
- Canonical URL appears in the final paragraph only

## X/Twitter — Visual-First

- The substance of the post lives in 1–4 platform-sized visuals commissioned via `visual-renderer` and validated by `visual-reviewer` (cross-model)
- The text is a single caption ≤ 240 characters that ends with the canonical URL
- Optional Unicode Mathematical Bold (𝗕𝗼𝗹𝗱) on a 2–4 word phrase only — never on a full sentence
- **Do NOT** write multi-tweet textual threads
- Single image: 16:9 (1600×900) or 1:1 (1200×1200); carousel (2–4): all 1:1
- At most 2 highly-relevant hashtags

## Reddit — Visual-First

- Submitted as an **Image Post** wherever the subreddit allows; the visual is the primary asset
- Standard Markdown ONLY for the accompanying text — no Unicode bold/italic
- Context paragraph: 2–4 sentences, ≤ 600 characters, ending with the canonical URL
- **No TL;DR section** — the visual is the TL;DR
- **No multi-section essay** — Reddit downvotes text walls that retread the image
- Provide 3–4 subreddit-specific title variants
- Conversational, anti-promotional tone

## Medium & Substack — Visual-First (Step 12)

- Generated by `platform-distiller` with one platform-sized hero visual each (commissioned via `visual-renderer`, PASS from `visual-reviewer`)
- Medium hero: 16:9 (1500×844). Substack hero: 1:1 (1200×1200) preferred, or 16:9 (1456×816)
- Body text: Medium 80–150 words, Substack 40–80 words
- Exactly one hero image per post — no inline screenshots, no extra figures
- Canonical URL appears at the end of the body
